title: Ch 9.1 — Try-Catch Basics: Evil Scheme Investment Tracker
description:
Practice using try-catch to handle exceptions when processing villain investment data.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Your Task
──────────────────────────────────────────────

Complete the EvilScheme class and the InvestmentTracker class below.

  CLASS: EvilScheme
  ─────────────────
  Fields (private):
    • String schemeName
    • String villainName
    • double cost

  Constructor:
    • EvilScheme(String schemeName, String villainName, double cost)

  Methods:
    • String getSchemeName()   — returns schemeName
    • String getVillainName()  — returns villainName
    • double getCost()         — returns cost
    • String displayInfo()     — returns:
        "Scheme: <name> by <villain> - Cost: $<cost>"
        example: "Scheme: Moon Theft by Gru - Cost: $1000000.0"

  CLASS: InvestmentTracker
  ─────────────────────────
  Field:
    • EvilScheme[] schemes  (size 10, some slots may be empty/null)
    • int count             (tracks how many schemes are stored)

  Methods to complete:

  (1) void addScheme(String name, String villain, String costText)
      • Use try-catch for NumberFormatException when parsing costText
      • If parsing fails, print:
          "Invalid cost for scheme '" + name + "': " + costText
        and do NOT add the scheme.
      • If valid, create an EvilScheme and add it to schemes[count],
        then increment count.

  (2) double calculateTotalInvestment()
      • Loop through all schemes (up to count)
      • Add up costs and return the total
      • Use try-catch: if any scheme is null, print
          "Warning: null scheme encountered"
        and skip it

  (3) String findMostExpensiveScheme()
      • Loop through schemes to find the highest cost
      • Return a String in the format:
          "<schemeName> by <villainName> ($<cost>)"
        example: "Pyramid Scheme by Vector ($2000000.0)"
      • If count == 0, return "No schemes tracked."

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• NumberFormatException is thrown by Double.parseDouble()
  when the input is not a valid number (e.g., "lots of money").
• Your catch block should print the message and then return
  (or just not add the scheme) — do NOT crash.
• The test passes costText as a String, so you always need to parse it.
